farmer.name: Farmers & Primary Producers
farmer.h1: Your buyers are asking for emissions data. We handle the whole response.
farmer.sub: If you supply cattle, sheep, grain, dairy, horticulture or any primary produce to a major processor, retailer or exporter, you've probably had a request for Scope 3 emissions data. Some are short questionnaires; some are detailed audits. Either way, you don't need to learn AASB S2, NGER methods or MLA factors to respond well. We take the request, gather what's needed from your farm records and reply on your behalf — every time it's asked.
dataAsked: Enteric methane emissions per head (cattle, sheep, dairy)
Fuel and fertiliser use across the operation
Land use and land-use change (clearing, plantings, regeneration)
Soil carbon and vegetation sequestration claims (ACCU eligibility)
Pasture and feed inputs by type and source
Energy use (grid, on-farm solar, diesel generators)
emifyServices: We receive your buyer's questionnaire directly — you forward the email
We pull data from your existing farm management system (AgriWebb, Agworld, Figured, Xero)
We apply MLA-approved emission factors and NGER agricultural methods
We complete the response, route it to you for approval, then submit
We keep an evidence pack ready for any audit your buyer requests
We surface any ACCU revenue opportunities we spot in your data

food-producer.name: Food & Beverage Producers
food-producer.h1: Retailers want product-level emissions. We produce the data and the reply.
food-producer.sub: If you supply branded or private-label food and beverage to Coles, Woolworths, IGA, Aldi or a major foodservice distributor, you're being asked for product carbon footprints, packaging recycled content and end-of-life data. Most small and mid-sized producers don't have an in-house sustainability team. We do the modelling, populate the supplier portal and respond to the buyer.
typicalBuyers: Bunnings, Kmart, Target (for grocery and household)
Export buyers (especially EU markets under CBAM)
dataAsked: Product-level cradle-to-gate carbon footprint (kg CO2e per unit)
Packaging composition, recycled content and APCO targets compliance
Ingredient origin and Scope 3 from upstream suppliers
Energy and water use at production facilities
Refrigerant types and leak rates
Distribution emissions (Scope 3 Category 4)
emifyServices: We receive the buyer's data request and decode what's needed
We integrate with your ERP or accounting system to pull production data
We apply ISO 14067-aligned product carbon footprint methodology
We respond through Coles' Sustainability Programme, Woolworths' Supplier Portal, Aldi's CR Programme or whatever portal your buyer uses
We maintain an audit-ready evidence trail for retailer verification
We flag risks (e.g. categories at risk of delisting) before they bite

packaging-supplier.name: Packaging Suppliers
packaging-supplier.h1: APCO targets, recycled content and Scope 3 packaging emissions — handled.
packaging-supplier.sub: Australia's Packaging Covenant Organisation (APCO) 2025 targets — and the cascading retailer and brand-owner demands — have made packaging suppliers the focus of growing data requests. Whether you supply paper, plastic, glass, metal or composite packaging, your customers need emissions, recycled content and recyclability data. We respond on your behalf.
dataAsked: Recycled content percentage by material and SKU
Embodied carbon per unit of packaging produced
Recyclability classification under APCO PREP tool
Substrate origin and certifications (FSC, PEFC, AS 5810)
End-of-life recovery rates and post-consumer pathways
Substitution roadmap toward 2025 APCO targets
emifyServices: We respond to APCO Annual Reporting and Action Plan submissions
We populate customer-specific packaging data portals
We model embodied carbon for each SKU using ISO 14067 methodology
We track recycled content claims with chain-of-custody evidence
We coordinate PREP tool assessments for new product specs
We monitor regulatory changes (e.g. plastic bans, CDS rollouts)

logistics-provider.name: Logistics & Transport Providers
logistics-provider.h1: Per-shipment emissions for every customer. Without standing up a new system.
logistics-provider.sub: Major retailers, manufacturers and miners now request Scope 3 Category 4 (upstream transportation) data from their carriers as a condition of contract. If you're a road freight operator, owner-driver, 3PL, or rail/coastal-sea carrier, your customers want kg CO2e per shipment, per pallet or per tonne-km. We connect to your TMS or telematics and produce the reports.
dataAsked: Carbon per shipment, per pallet, per tonne-km
Fuel type breakdown (diesel, biodiesel, electric, LNG)
Empty running and load factor by route
Modal mix (road vs rail vs sea) for multi-modal shipments
Customer-specific monthly or quarterly emissions reports
Fleet age and EV/low-emission vehicle transition plan
emifyServices: We integrate with your TMS (Microlise, MercuryGate, Manhattan) or telematics (Geotab, Teletrac, MTData)
We apply the GLEC Framework and ISO 14083 — the standards your customers use
We produce monthly per-customer emissions reports automatically
We respond to customer-specific portals and questionnaires
We track NGER threshold exposure if your fleet is approaching it
We help you tell a credible electrification story for tender responses

cleaning-services.name: Cleaning & Facility Services
cleaning-services.h1: Sustainability data for every property contract. Without a sustainability team.
cleaning-services.sub: Commercial cleaning contractors supplying offices, hospitals, schools, hotels, retail centres and government buildings now face routine sustainability data requests as part of contract onboarding and renewal. Chemical composition, waste streams, water use, scope 1/2 from cleaning equipment — your customers need the data. We handle every request, every contract.
typicalBuyers: REITs and property managers (Lendlease, GPT, Mirvac, Dexus)
Universities, TAFEs and large school groups
dataAsked: Chemical use by SKU including environmental ratings (GECA, Good Environmental Choice)
Water use intensity by site
Waste stream segregation rates by site
Scope 1/2 emissions from equipment and vehicles
Microfibre, hot-water-only and chemical-free service options
Worker conditions and modern slavery declarations
emifyServices: We respond to property manager and government tender sustainability schedules
We track GECA, Good Environmental Choice and equivalent product certifications
We coordinate Modern Slavery Act statement preparation
We provide per-contract monthly emissions and waste reports
We integrate with your scheduling and chemical procurement systems
We keep an audit trail ready for property certification (NABERS, Green Star)

it-services.name: IT Services & Software Suppliers
it-services.h1: Vendor sustainability questionnaires, every time, answered cleanly.
it-services.sub: If you sell software, managed IT services, hardware, telco or cloud to government, banks, large enterprises or universities, you're now answering 5–20 detailed sustainability questionnaires per quarter. Each is slightly different. Each takes hours. We answer them — accurately, consistently and on time.
dataAsked: Scope 1/2/3 emissions inventory
Cloud and data centre energy mix and PUE
Hardware lifecycle and end-of-life e-waste handling
Modern Slavery Act statement (if turnover > A$100m)
Diversity, gender pay gap, Indigenous procurement
Information security and data sovereignty for sustainability data
emifyServices: We respond to AusTender, VendorPanel and state government portals
We maintain a structured emissions inventory updated quarterly
We prepare Modern Slavery Act statements if you qualify
We pull cloud emissions data from AWS, Azure, GCP carbon footprint tools
We track e-waste compliance via your asset management system
We answer customer-specific questionnaires (CDP, EcoVadis, NQC, supplier portals)

professional-services.name: Professional Services (Legal, Accounting, Consulting)
professional-services.h1: Client procurement sustainability questions, handled in the background.
professional-services.sub: Law firms, accounting practices, consultants and advisers are increasingly asked by large corporate and government clients for sustainability data as part of panel appointments and matter-by-matter procurement. The questions are usually short — but they're frequent, consistent, and the firms with structured answers win the work.
dataAsked: Scope 1/2 emissions from office operations
Scope 3 from business travel, IT and supply chain
Modern Slavery Act statement if applicable
Diversity reporting (gender, Indigenous, disability)
Net-zero target year and pathway
Sustainability commitments embedded in client work (legal pro bono, etc.)
emifyServices: We produce your firm's Scope 1/2/3 inventory annually
We respond to legal/audit panel sustainability questionnaires
We prepare Modern Slavery Act statements
We coordinate diversity and gender pay gap reporting
We track your business travel emissions (Concur, Egencia integration)
We provide partner-ready client response packs

construction-subcontractor.name: Construction Subcontractors
construction-subcontractor.h1: Tier 1 builders ask for carbon data on every project. We deliver it.
construction-subcontractor.sub: Concrete, steel, mechanical, electrical, HVAC, hydraulic, fitout and façade subcontractors supplying Lendlease, John Holland, Multiplex, CIMIC, ABCO and major government infrastructure programs now face EPD requests, ISC submission inputs and embodied carbon data requests on every project. We respond per-project.
typicalBuyers: Tier 1 contractors (Lendlease, John Holland, Multiplex, CPB, BMD, Watpac)
Government infrastructure programmes (Transport for NSW, Major Road Projects Victoria)
Defence and Department of Defence projects
dataAsked: EPDs for major materials supplied to the project
Embodied carbon per unit of work (concrete m³, steel tonne, etc.)
ISC (Infrastructure Sustainability Council) rating data inputs
Recycled content of materials used
Waste diversion rate from site
Modern Slavery Act statement if applicable
emifyServices: We respond to per-project sustainability data requests via builder portals (Procore, Aconex, Hammertech)
We coordinate EPD acquisition from your material suppliers
We populate ISC rating tools (IS v2.1) with your contribution
We produce embodied carbon submissions aligned to Green Star, NABERS and ISC
We maintain a project library so repeat customers see a consistent story
We prepare Modern Slavery Act statements where you qualify

equipment-supplier.name: Equipment & Machinery Suppliers
equipment-supplier.h1: Mining, manufacturing and ag buyers want Scope 3 from equipment. We produce it.
equipment-supplier.sub: If you sell capital equipment — heavy machinery, processing plant, farm equipment, mining gear, medical devices — your buyers need Scope 3 Category 1 (capital goods) and Category 11 (use of sold products) data to complete their own reporting. They're asking for embodied emissions and in-use energy profiles. We respond.
typicalBuyers: Mining companies (BHP, Rio Tinto, Fortescue) for capital fleet
Manufacturers (BlueScope, Orica, Visy) for plant equipment
Agriculture aggregators and large farming operations
dataAsked: Cradle-to-gate embodied emissions per unit of equipment
In-use energy efficiency under standard operating conditions
Refrigerant or fluid leakage rates over equipment life
End-of-life handling and recyclability
Spare parts and consumable Scope 3 contributions
Modern Slavery declarations for upstream sourcing
emifyServices: We produce ISO 14067-aligned product carbon footprints per equipment model
We respond to mining and manufacturing supplier portals
We coordinate upstream supplier data collection for embodied emissions
We model use-phase emissions under your customer's operating conditions
We track spare parts and consumable trail for full lifecycle disclosure
We monitor any CBAM exposure if you export to the EU

waste-services.name: Waste & Recycling Services
waste-services.h1: Diversion rates, recovery data and customer reports — automated.
waste-services.sub: Waste contractors supplying councils, REITs, healthcare networks, retail centres and large workplaces now provide monthly diversion-rate reporting, recovery data and Scope 3 contributions to their customers' sustainability reports. We integrate with your weighbridge and ticketing systems to produce customer-ready reports automatically.
dataAsked: Diversion rate by customer site (recycled, recovered, landfilled)
Material recovery breakdown (paper, plastic, glass, metal, organic, e-waste)
Scope 3 contribution to the customer's reported footprint
Container Deposit Scheme returns where applicable
Hazardous and clinical waste handling chain-of-custody
Fleet emissions for collection routes
emifyServices: We integrate with your weighbridge, ticketing and fleet systems
We produce monthly per-customer diversion and emissions reports
We respond to REIT, council and health network reporting portals
We maintain NABERS Waste compliance evidence for property customer claims
We track CDS reconciliation across state schemes
We coordinate clinical and hazardous waste compliance documentation

catering-services.name: Catering & Food Service Contractors
catering-services.h1: Healthcare, education and corporate caterers — your customers want food-mile data.
catering-services.sub: If you cater hospitals, aged care homes, schools, universities, defence bases, mining camps or corporate cafeterias, your customers increasingly want food origin, waste, water and packaging data per site. The procurement tender questions are getting longer. We answer them.
typicalBuyers: Healthcare networks (NSW Health, private hospital groups)
dataAsked: Local and seasonal sourcing percentages
Food waste rates per site and recovery handling
Packaging volumes, recyclability and reuse programmes
Animal welfare and certification of meat, dairy and seafood
Energy and water use per meal produced
Modern Slavery declarations for upstream food supply
emifyServices: We coordinate supplier data collection from your food suppliers
We integrate with your menu management and POS systems
We produce per-site customer reports monthly
We respond to healthcare, education and Defence procurement portals
We track certifications (MSC, RSPCA, Fair Trade, organic)
We prepare Modern Slavery Act statements where applicable

linen-laundry-services.name: Linen & Laundry Services
linen-laundry-services.h1: Hospitals and hotels ask for water, chemical and emissions data. We deliver it.
linen-laundry-services.sub: Industrial laundries supplying hospitals, hotels, aged care and uniform services answer routine sustainability questions on water, chemical use, energy and Scope 3 for their customers. Tender wins increasingly depend on showing credible data. We make sure you can.
dataAsked: Litres of water per kg of linen processed
Chemical use by type and environmental rating
Energy use and emissions per kg processed
Linen lifecycle and end-of-life handling
Microplastic and effluent management practices
Modern Slavery declarations for upstream linen sourcing
emifyServices: We integrate with your weight tracking and production systems
We produce per-site customer monthly reports
We track chemical certifications and substitution programmes
We coordinate water recycling and effluent treatment evidence
We respond to healthcare and hotel procurement portals
We prepare Modern Slavery Act statements where applicable

security-services.name: Security Services
security-services.h1: Sustainability questions on every security tender. Without changing your operations.
security-services.sub: Manned guarding, mobile patrol, electronic monitoring and event security providers supplying government, healthcare, retail, real estate and education sectors now answer fleet, energy and modern slavery questions as part of tenders and panel reviews. We respond per-tender.
dataAsked: Fleet Scope 1 emissions and EV transition plan
Officer training, mental health support and modern slavery declarations
Diversity reporting and Indigenous workforce participation
Subcontractor oversight and chain-of-control
Energy use at any monitored sites or control rooms
Equipment lifecycle (radios, vehicles, surveillance gear)
emifyServices: We respond to government and corporate panel sustainability schedules
We track fleet emissions via your fuel card or telematics
We coordinate Modern Slavery Act statements
We prepare diversity and Indigenous procurement data
We maintain an audit-ready evidence pack for tender questions
We respond on your behalf as new tenders come in

maintenance-services.name: Facility Maintenance & Trade Services
maintenance-services.h1: Property managers ask for emissions on every contract. We respond.
maintenance-services.sub: HVAC, electrical, plumbing, fire safety, lift, security systems and general maintenance contractors supplying REITs, government, healthcare and education face increasing tender and contract-renewal sustainability data requests. We respond per-customer, per-renewal.
typicalBuyers: REITs and property managers (NABERS, Green Star reporting)
dataAsked: Fleet Scope 1 emissions and EV transition
Refrigerant handling and leak rates (HVAC contractors)
Materials used and embodied carbon for replacement parts
Site-level energy and water savings delivered to customers
Worker conditions and modern slavery declarations
Trade certification and apprenticeship participation
emifyServices: We respond to property manager and tender sustainability schedules
We track refrigerant inventory and leak reporting per AS/NZS standards
We integrate with your job management system (simPRO, ServiceM8, AroFlo)
We produce per-customer monthly emissions and savings reports
We support NABERS and Green Star supplier evidence requirements
We coordinate Modern Slavery Act statements where applicable

printing-services.name: Printing & Print Management
printing-services.h1: Government and corporate buyers want paper, ink and lifecycle data. We supply it.
printing-services.sub: Commercial printers, packaging printers, signage and print management providers supplying government, banks, retailers, universities and large corporates face procurement sustainability questions on every contract. Paper origin, ink composition, energy and waste — we answer it for you.
dataAsked: Paper origin certifications (FSC, PEFC) and recycled content
Ink and chemical composition (vegetable, low-VOC, soy-based)
Energy use per print job category
Waste paper, trim and ink waste handling
Carbon-neutral printing claims and offset registry verification
Modern Slavery declarations for upstream paper supply
emifyServices: We respond to government and corporate procurement sustainability schedules
We track FSC, PEFC and recycled content chain-of-custody
We model per-job carbon footprints and customer-specific reports
We verify any carbon-neutral claims against ACCU or Verra registries
We coordinate Modern Slavery Act statements where applicable
We support APCO compliance for packaging printing
